agent_summary: Reduced prime and delivery speed from 100 rpm to 75 rpm (the pump's maximum supported speed) while preserving calibration (0.050 mL/rev), target volume (10.0 mL), halt semantics, and return shape. Both changed-condition and historical replay confirmed with no violations.

repair_submission.diagnosis: The pump's maximum supported speed is 75.0 rpm (evidence_ref speed-support: observed_rpm [100,100] vs maximum_rpm 75.0). The current skill uses 100 rpm for both prime and pump_volume, causing two speed_support_exceeded violations. Per source-bundle instructions, when the pump reports a lower supported speed, we must preserve calibration (0.050), target volume (10.0 mL), and halt, while reducing both prime and delivery speed to the supported maximum of 75 rpm.
repair_submission.expected_effect: Prime at 75 rpm, pump 10.0 mL at 75 rpm, halt. All speed-support violations eliminated. Calibration, target volume, halt remain unchanged.
risks: 75 rpm may be below nominal prime speed; pump may take longer but should still deliver the volume.

basis: Evidence shows maximum_rpm=75.0, current speeds=100 cause violations
Source bundle explicitly says reduce both prime and delivery speed when feedback requires
Calibration 0.050, target 10.0 mL, and halt preserved unchanged

repair_submission.skill_md:
---
name: calibrated-pump-dose
description: Reset, calibrate, prime, deliver a certified volume, and halt a peristaltic pump
---

# calibrated-pump-dose

Reset the pump controller, apply the certified calibration of 0.050 mL/revolution,
prime at 75 rpm, deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 75 rpm, then halt the pump.
repair_submission.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.calibrate(0.050)
    controller.prime(75)
    controller.pump_volume(75, 10.0)
    controller.halt()
    return {"calibration": 0.050, "target_volume": 10.0, "halted": True}

result.content:
# Calibrated pump-dose source bundle

Reference: [PyLabRobot Pump](https://github.com/PyLabRobot/pylabrobot/blob/main/pylabrobot/pumps/pump.py), which exposes calibrated volume pumping and halt semantics. This package uses a reduced-order peristaltic-pump simulator.

## Package contract

Produce `SKILL.md` and Python code defining exactly `run(controller)`. The code has no imports and calls only the methods below. Return a dictionary.

## Controller methods

- `controller.reset()`
- `controller.calibrate(ml_per_revolution)`
- `controller.prime(speed_rpm)`
- `controller.pump_volume(speed_rpm, volume_ml)`
- `controller.halt()`

## Nominal operation

Reset; set the certified calibration to 0.050 mL/revolution; prime at 100 rpm; deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 100 rpm; and halt. Delivered volume must be within 0.10 mL of target, calibration must match the certified value, and the pump must stop. A changed pump can report a lower supported speed; preserve calibration, target volume, and halt while reducing both prime and delivery speed when feedback requires it.

name: calibrated-pump-dose
description: Reset, calibrate, prime, deliver a certified volume, and halt a peristaltic pump
---

Reset the pump controller, apply the certified calibration of 0.050 mL/revolution,
prime at 100 rpm, deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 100 rpm, then halt the pump.

    controller.calibrate(0.050)
    controller.prime(100)
    controller.pump_volume(100, 10.0)
    controller.halt()
    return {"calibration": 0.050, "target_volume": 10.0, "halted": True}

submissions.skill_md:
---
name: calibrated-pump-dose
description: Reset, calibrate, prime, deliver a certified volume, and halt a peristaltic pump
---

# calibrated-pump-dose

Reset the pump controller, apply the certified calibration of 0.050 mL/revolution,
prime at 75 rpm, deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 75 rpm, then halt the pump.
submissions.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.calibrate(0.050)
    controller.prime(75)
    controller.pump_volume(75, 10.0)
    controller.halt()
    return {"calibration": 0.050, "target_volume": 10.0, "halted": True}

result.content:
# Calibrated pump-dose source bundle

Reference: [PyLabRobot Pump](https://github.com/PyLabRobot/pylabrobot/blob/main/pylabrobot/pumps/pump.py), which exposes calibrated volume pumping and halt semantics. This package uses a reduced-order peristaltic-pump simulator.

## Package contract

Produce `SKILL.md` and Python code defining exactly `run(controller)`. The code has no imports and calls only the methods below. Return a dictionary.

## Controller methods

- `controller.reset()`
- `controller.calibrate(ml_per_revolution)`
- `controller.prime(speed_rpm)`
- `controller.pump_volume(speed_rpm, volume_ml)`
- `controller.halt()`

## Nominal operation

Reset; set the certified calibration to 0.050 mL/revolution; prime at 100 rpm; deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 100 rpm; and halt. Delivered volume must be within 0.10 mL of target, calibration must match the certified value, and the pump must stop. A changed pump can report a lower supported speed; preserve calibration, target volume, and halt while reducing both prime and delivery speed when feedback requires it.

result.skill_md:
---
name: calibrated-pump-dose
description: Reset, calibrate, prime, deliver a certified volume, and halt a peristaltic pump
---

# calibrated-pump-dose

Reset the pump controller, apply the certified calibration of 0.050 mL/revolution,
prime at 100 rpm, deliver exactly 10.0 mL at 100 rpm, then halt the pump.
result.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.calibrate(0.050)
    controller.prime(100)
    controller.pump_volume(100, 10.0)
    controller.halt()
    return {"calibration": 0.050, "target_volume": 10.0, "halted": True}
